Legislative Assembly elections were held in the Indian state of [[Elections in Uttar Pradesh|Uttar Pradesh]] from [[2022 elections in India|10 February to 7 March 2022]] in seven phases to elect all [[List of constituencies of the Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly|403 members]] for the [[18th Uttar Pradesh Assembly|18th Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly]]. The votes were counted and the results were declared on 10 March 2022.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Singh |first1=Karan Deep |last2=Mashal |first2=Mujib |date=10 March 2022 |title=Modi's Party Wins the Biggest Prize in India's State Elections |language=en-US |work=The New York Times |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/10/world/asia/india-elections-modi.html |access-date=27 April 2022 |issn=0362-4331 |archive-date=27 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220427080559/https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/10/world/asia/india-elections-modi.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

== Background ==
The tenure of [[Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly]] was scheduled to end on 14 May 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Terms of the Houses |url=https://eci.gov.in/elections/term-of-houses/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211117044324/https://eci.gov.in/elections/term-of-houses/ |archive-date=17 November 2021 |access-date=4 October 2021 |website=Election Commission of India |language=en-IN}}</ref> The [[2017 Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly election|previous assembly elections]] were held February–March 2017. After the election, [[Bharatiya Janata Party]] formed the [[Government of Uttar Pradesh|state government]], with [[Yogi Adityanath]] becoming [[List of chief ministers of Uttar Pradesh|Chief Minister]].<ref>{{cite web |date=19 March 2017 |editor-last=Chakraborty |editor-first=Ananya |others=PTI |title=Yogi Adityanath Takes Oath as Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister |url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/yogi-adityanath-takes-oath-as-uttar-pradesh-chief-minister-1361750.html |access-date=8 January 2022 |website=News18 |language=en |archive-date=8 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220108124028/https://www.news18.com/news/politics/yogi-adityanath-takes-oath-as-uttar-pradesh-chief-minister-1361750.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

=== Panchayat Elections ===

In the 2021 Uttar Pradesh Panchayat Elections, [[Samajwadi Party|SP]] won 760 wards, followed by [[BJP]] with 720 wards. [[Bahujan Samaj Party]] won 381 and [[Indian National Congress]] won 76 wards. Independents and smaller parties won in 1,114 wards.<ref name="UP panchayat elections HT">{{cite news |last=Singh |first=Rajesh Kumar |date=5 May 2021 |title=Setback for BJP in key areas in UP panchayat elections |language=en |work=Hindustan Times |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/spmakes-gains-bjp-jolted-in-up-panchayat-polls-101620154099505.html |access-date=12 February 2022 |archive-date=12 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220212140844/https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/spmakes-gains-bjp-jolted-in-up-panchayat-polls-101620154099505.html |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Aam Aadmi Party|AAP]]  won 64 and [[All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen|AIMIM]] won 22 wards in the panchayat elections.<ref>{{cite news |title=UP Panchayat Elections: AAP Wins One Seat Each in Varanasi, Gorakhpur; AIMIM Raises its Graph |url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/up-panchayat-elections-aap-wins-one-seat-each-in-varanasi-gorakhpur-while-aimim-raises-its-graph-3712574.html |access-date=10 February 2022 |work=News18 |date=6 May 2021 |language=en |archive-date=10 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220210193558/https://www.news18.com/news/politics/up-panchayat-elections-aap-wins-one-seat-each-in-varanasi-gorakhpur-while-aimim-raises-its-graph-3712574.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Political developments===

In January 2022, ten [[Bharatiya Janata Party|BJP]] state legislators including three ministers, left the party and joined [[Samajwadi Party]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Biswas |first=Soutik |date=14 January 2022 |title=Uttar Pradesh elections: Is a rebellion brewing in Modi's BJP? |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-59979808 |access-date=15 January 2022 |archive-date=15 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220115054805/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-59979808 |url-status=live }}</ref> On 19 January, [[Mulayam Singh Yadav]]'s daughter-in-law Aparna Bisht Yadav joined BJP.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Mathew |first=Liz |date=19 January 2022 |title=Uttar Pradesh elections: Aparna Yadav joins BJP. |language=en-GB |work=The Indian Express |url=https://indianexpress.com/elections/aparna-yadav-sp-bjp-mulayam-singh-daughter-in-law-up-7731161/lite/ |access-date=2 February 2022 |archive-date=2 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220202124418/https://indianexpress.com/elections/aparna-yadav-sp-bjp-mulayam-singh-daughter-in-law-up-7731161/lite/ |url-status=live }}</ref> She was followed by Mulayam Singh's brother-in-law Pramod Gupta who joined BJP on 20 January.<ref>{{Cite news|date=20 January 2022|title=Uttar Pradesh elections: Pramod Gupta joins BJP.|language=en-GB|work=The Times of India|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/up-elections-2022-samajwadi-party-patriarch-mulayam-singh-yadavs-brother-in-law-pramod-gupta-joins-bjp/articleshow/89012860.cms|access-date=2 February 2022|archive-date=17 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230417165759/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/up-elections-2022-samajwadi-party-patriarch-mulayam-singh-yadavs-brother-in-law-pramod-gupta-joins-bjp/articleshow/89012860.cms|url-status=live}}</ref> On 25 January, former Union Minister and Congress leader [[Ratanjit Pratap Narain Singh]] joined BJP.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Hebbar |first=Nistula |date=25 January 2022 |title=R.P.N. Singh joins BJP, party says boost to prospects in eastern U.P. |language=en-IN |work=The Hindu |url=https://www.thehindu.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly/rpn-singh-resigns-from-congress-all-set-to-join-bjp/article38323169.ece |access-date=3 March 2022 |issn=0971-751X |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220140845/https://www.thehindu.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly/rpn-singh-resigns-from-congress-all-set-to-join-bjp/article38323169.ece |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Schedule==
The election schedule was announced by the [[Election Commission of India]] on 8 January 2022.<ref>{{cite web |last=Gupta |first=Shubhangi |date=8 January 2022 |title=Assembly elections 2022: Check complete schedule for Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Goa, Manipur & Punjab |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/assembly-election-2022-date-check-complete-polling-schedule-for-uttar-pradesh-uttarakhand-goa-manipur-punjab-101641639532618.html |access-date=8 January 2022 |website=Hindustan Times |language=en |archive-date=8 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220108120002/https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/assembly-election-2022-date-check-complete-polling-schedule-for-uttar-pradesh-uttarakhand-goa-manipur-punjab-101641639532618.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

== Parties and alliances ==

=== National Democratic Alliance ===
<!-- Per [[MOS:NOSECTIONLINKS]]: for technical reasons, section headings should, among other things, not contain links, citations, or template transclusions. -->
{{legend2|{{party color|National Democratic Alliance}}|[[National Democratic Alliance]]}}

During the month of September, the NDA confirmed an alliance between BJP, AD(S) and the [[NISHAD Party]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Our aim is to ensure NDA's victory in UP election, says Sanjay Nishad |url=https://www.aninews.in/news/national/general-news/our-aim-is-to-ensure-ndas-victory-in-up-election-says-sanjay-nishad20210928095903/ |access-date=5 December 2021 |website=ANI News |language=en |archive-date=5 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211205214358/https://www.aninews.in/news/national/general-news/our-aim-is-to-ensure-ndas-victory-in-up-election-says-sanjay-nishad20210928095903/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=NDA alliance takes shape in Uttar Pradesh; BJP, Nishad party and Apna Dal to contest upcoming Assembly polls |url=https://www.tribuneindia.com/news/nation/uttar-pradesh-nda-alliance-takes-shape-bjp-nishad-party-and-apna-dal-to-contest-the-upcoming-assembly-elections-315639 |access-date=5 December 2021 |website=Tribuneindia News Service |language=en |archive-date=6 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211206070834/https://www.tribuneindia.com/news/nation/uttar-pradesh-nda-alliance-takes-shape-bjp-nishad-party-and-apna-dal-to-contest-the-upcoming-assembly-elections-315639 |url-status=live}}</ref> During the month of August, the NDA held talks with parties like [[Janata Dal (United)|JD(U)]], [[Hindustani Awam Morcha|HAM(S)]]<ref>{{cite web |date=16 November 2021 |first=Himanshu |last=Mishra |title=JD(U) likely to contest Uttar Pradesh election in alliance with BJP in 2022 |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jdu-likely-contest-uttar-pradesh-election-alliance-bjp-2022-1877097-2021-11-16 |access-date=5 December 2021 |website=India Today |language=en |archive-date=17 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211117170805/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jdu-likely-contest-uttar-pradesh-election-alliance-bjp-2022-1877097-2021-11-16 |url-status=live}}</ref> and others, however the seat sharing talks fell apart later. In October, there were major restructuring efforts by the alliance with new faces and revamp of parties in an effort to battle anti-incumbency. In the first 2 weeks of December, the alliance launched it campaign for the election.<ref>{{Cite news |title=UP Assembly election 2022 : Top BJP leaders to hold 6 rallies next week |work=The Economic Times |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/up-assembly-election-2022-top-bjp-leaders-to-hold-6-rallies-next-week/articleshow/88094554.cms |access-date=5 December 2021 |archive-date=4 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211204150236/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/up-assembly-election-2022-top-bjp-leaders-to-hold-6-rallies-next-week/articleshow/88094554.cms |url-status=live}}</ref> On 13 Jan the national democratic alliance sealed their seat sharing pact with NISHAD Party getting 16 and Apna Dal getting 17 and BJP competing on remaining 370 seats. 6 NISHAD Party candidates would fight on BJP symbol.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Known for Its Meteoric Rise, Is UP's Nishad Party Set for Success This Time? |url=https://thewire.in/politics/known-for-its-meteoric-rise-can-ups-nishad-party-repeat-the-feat-this-time |access-date=7 March 2022 |website=The Wire |archive-date=24 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220224111852/https://thewire.in/politics/known-for-its-meteoric-rise-can-ups-nishad-party-repeat-the-feat-this-time |url-status=live}}</ref>[[File:NDA candidacies for 2022 Uttar Pradesh elections.png|thumb|155px|Map of the seat sharing arrangement between the parties of the NDA for the 2022 Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly election.]]

=== Samajwadi Party+ ===
<!-- Per [[MOS:NOSECTIONLINKS]]: for technical reasons, section headings should, among other things, not contain links, citations, or template transclusions. -->
RLD was the first to join the alliance. The NCP and RJD too joined the alliance later.<ref>{{cite web |date=27 July 2021 |title=NCP to tie up with SP for UP assembly polls |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/ncp-to-tie-up-with-sp-for-up-assembly-polls-101627408459880.html |access-date=4 December 2021 |website=Hindustan Times |language=en |archive-date=4 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211204215852/https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/ncp-to-tie-up-with-sp-for-up-assembly-polls-101627408459880.html |url-status=live}}</ref> Various other smaller parties too joined while SBSP broke away from its alliance to join SP alliance.<ref>{{Cite news |title=SP-SBSP join hands, give slogan 'Khadeda Hobe' on lines of Mamata's 'Khela Hobe' battle cry |work=The Economic Times |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/sp-sbsp-join-hands-give-slogan-khadeda-hobe-on-lines-of-mamatas-khela-hobe-battle-cry/articleshow/87314480.cms?from=mdr |access-date=4 December 2021 |archive-date=4 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211204215851/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/sp-sbsp-join-hands-give-slogan-khadeda-hobe-on-lines-of-mamatas-khela-hobe-battle-cry/articleshow/87314480.cms?from=mdr |url-status=live}}</ref> During the first seat sharing talks, SP agreed to give RLD 36 seats. Initially, RLD demanded 60 seats while SP were willing to give up to 30, later both the parties finalised at 33 with RLD mostly competing in [[Western Uttar Pradesh|West UP]]. RLD gave 9 symbol of SP candidates.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Anshuman |first=Kumar |title=SP, RLD strike poll alliance |work=The Economic Times |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/sp-rld-strike-poll-alliance/articleshow/87839894.cms |access-date=4 December 2021 |archive-date=4 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211204215851/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/sp-rld-strike-poll-alliance/articleshow/87839894.cms |url-status=live}}</ref> Aam Aadmi Party and Samajwadi Party began talks for alliance,<ref>{{cite web |date=24 November 2021 |first=Kumar |last=Abhishek |title=AAP heading for alliance with SP, Sanjay Singh says after meeting Akhilesh Yadav |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/aap-samajwadi-party-alliance-sanjay-singh-arvind-kejriwal-akhilesh-yadav-1880244-2021-11-24 |access-date=5 December 2021 |website=India Today |language=en |archive-date=30 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211130002601/https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/aap-samajwadi-party-alliance-sanjay-singh-arvind-kejriwal-akhilesh-yadav-1880244-2021-11-24 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=25 November 2021 |title=AAP, SP discuss seat-sharing for UP polls |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/lucknow/aap-samajwadi-party-alliance-up-elections-7639022/ |access-date=7 December 2021 |website=The Indian Express |language=en |archive-date=6 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211206160329/https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/lucknow/aap-samajwadi-party-alliance-up-elections-7639022/ |url-status=live}}</ref> however they couldn't agree on seat sharing.<ref>{{Cite news |title=AAP-SP deadlock over seat sharing, AAP to go it alone in UP |work=The Economic Times |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/aap-sp-deadlock-over-seat-sharing-aap-to-go-it-alone-in-up/articleshow/88274708.cms?from=mdr |access-date=17 December 2021 |archive-date=17 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211217104158/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/aap-sp-deadlock-over-seat-sharing-aap-to-go-it-alone-in-up/articleshow/88274708.cms?from=mdr |url-status=live}}</ref> Pragatisheel Samajwadi Party (Lohiya) joined the alliance later. On 13 January 2022, the alliance announced its initial candidates for the first few phases of the election. SP and SBSP would have a friendly fight on 1 seat while SP and AD(K) would have a friendly fight on 2 seats.{{Citation needed|date=March 2022}}[[File:Samajwadi+ alliance candidates (2022 Uttar Pradesh elections).png|thumb|155px|Map of the seat sharing arrangement between the parties of the SP+ for the 2022 Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly election.]]

=== Bahujan Samaj Party ===
<!-- Per [[MOS:NOSECTIONLINKS]]: for technical reasons, section headings should, among other things, not contain links, citations, or template transclusions. -->
Unlike in previous years, the [[Bahujan Samaj Party]] announced that it would compete in the election all by itself.<ref name=":0" /> BSP went into an alliance with ten small political parties, namely the India Janshakti Party, Pacchasi Parivartan Samaj Party, Vishwa Shanti Party, Sanyukt Janadesh Party, Adarsh Sangram Party, Akhand Vikas Bharat Party, Sarvajan Awaz Party, Jagruk Janata Party and Sarvajan Sewa Party for their extended support to BSP.<ref>{{cite web |last=Tiwari |first=Umesh |title=यूपी चुनाव 2022: विकास के लिए बसपा प्रमुख मायावती के साथ आए 10 राजनीतिक दल, समर्थन का किया ऐलान |website=Dainik Jagran |date=18 January 2022 |url=https://www.jagran.com/uttar-pradesh/lucknow-city-up-vidhan-sabha-election-10-political-parties-came-with-bsp-chief-mayawati-for-development-announced-support-22393621.html |language=hi |access-date=22 January 2022 |archive-date=22 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220122124350/https://www.jagran.com/uttar-pradesh/lucknow-city-up-vidhan-sabha-election-10-political-parties-came-with-bsp-chief-mayawati-for-development-announced-support-22393621.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=19 January 2022 |title=Nine Parties Extend Support To Mayawati |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/nine-parties-extend-support-to-mayawati/articleshow/88984110.cms |access-date=22 January 2022 |website=The Times of India |archive-date=22 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220122124350/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/nine-parties-extend-support-to-mayawati/articleshow/88984110.cms |url-status=live}}</ref>

=== Others ===
During the month leading up to the elections major political parties which aren't part of any alliance announced their intentions to compete in the election.{{Citation needed|date=March 2022}}
* [[Aam Aadmi Party|AAP]] announced that it would compete on all 403 seats but later contested on 349 seats including [[Charthawal Assembly constituency]] where the AAP candidate joined SP on poll eve. AAP started an alliance discussion with [[Samajwadi Party|SP]] but the talks for alliance did not succeed.<ref>{{Cite web |title=AAP candidate from Charthawal constituency joins Samajwadi Party on UP poll eve |url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2022/feb/09/aap-candidate-from-charthawal-constituency-joins-samajwadi-party-on-up-poll-eve-2417442.html |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=The New Indian Express |date=9 February 2022 |archive-date=15 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220415143311/https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2022/feb/09/aap-candidate-from-charthawal-constituency-joins-samajwadi-party-on-up-poll-eve-2417442.html |url-status=live}}</ref>
* [[Shiv Sena]] announced they would compete all 403 seat in the election which was later reduced to 36 seats.
* [[All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen|AIMIM]] originally was part of alliance and was given a seat share of 100 seats, however when [[Suheldev Bharatiya Samaj Party|SBSP]] broke the alliance to join hands with [[Samajwadi Party|SP]], [[All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen|AIMIM]] confirmed they would fight the election alone in 95 seats.

Later parties like [[Vikassheel Insaan Party|VSIP]], [[Lok Janshakti Party (Ram Vilas)|LJP (Ram Vilas)]], Rashtriya Rashtrawadi Party, [[Hindu Mahasabha|ABHM]] and [[Azad Samaj Party|ASPKR]] also confirmed their participation in the election<ref>{{Cite web |date=28 January 2012 |first=Arvind |last=Chauhan |title=Former Commando In Mulayam, Shivpal Security To Contest From Lucknow Cantt |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/former-commando-in-mulayam-shivpal-security-to-contest-from-lucknow-cantt/articleshow/89167969.cms |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=The Times of India |language=en |archive-date=11 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220611210533/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/former-commando-in-mulayam-shivpal-security-to-contest-from-lucknow-cantt/articleshow/89167969.cms |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite web |date=23 December 2021 |title=LJP (Ram Vilas) to go it alone in UP Assembly polls |url=https://www.thestatesman.com/uttar-pradesh/ljp-ram-vilas-go-alone-assembly-polls-1503032636.html |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=The Statesman |language=en-US |archive-date=11 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220611204340/https://www.thestatesman.com/uttar-pradesh/ljp-ram-vilas-go-alone-assembly-polls-1503032636.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=11 March 2022 |title=Bhim Army's Chandra Shekhar Azad loses to Yogi in Gorakhpur, forfeits deposit |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election/up-election-results-bhim-army-s-chandra-shekhar-azad-loses-to-yogi-in-gorakhpur-101646975404754.html |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=Hindustan Times |language=en |archive-date=12 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220612045957/https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election/up-election-results-bhim-army-s-chandra-shekhar-azad-loses-to-yogi-in-gorakhpur-101646975404754.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

==Candidates==
{{Main|List of candidates in the 2022 Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly election}}

==Manifestos==

=== NDA ===
On 8 February, BJP released its manifesto titled "''Lok Sankalp Patra''", ahead of the 1st phase of the elections.<ref>{{cite web|last=Singh|first=Purna|date=8 February 2022|title=BJP releases its election manifesto for UP election 2022 under the name 'Lok Kalyan Sankalp Patra'|url=https://www.thehansindia.com/news/national/bjp-releases-its-election-manifesto-for-up-election-2022-under-the-name-lok-kalyan-sankalp-patra-728335|access-date=10 February 2022|website=www.thehansindia.com|language=en|archive-date=10 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220210065437/https://www.thehansindia.com/news/national/bjp-releases-its-election-manifesto-for-up-election-2022-under-the-name-lok-kalyan-sankalp-patra-728335|url-status=live}}</ref> Some of the promises in it being:

* [[Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi]] (PMKISAN) scheme for landless farmers
* [[Scooty]] for female students and working women
* Free coaching to female students preparing for [[Civil Services Examination|UPSC]] and PSC
* Laptops and other gadgets needed for [[Distance education|online education]] for students
* Give at least 1 job to each household
* 3 free [[Liquefied petroleum gas|LPG]] cylinders to consumers each year under [[Ujjwala Yojana|Ujjwala Yojna]]

===BSP===
BSP announced that it will not release election manifesto.<ref name="Manifesto not released">{{cite news|date=13 January 2022|title=Main parties in Uttar Pradesh yet to release manifestos|language=en-us|work=Hindustan Times|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/main-parties-in-uttar-pradesh-yet-to-release-manifestos-101642014353601.html|access-date=7 February 2022|archive-date=7 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220207073530/https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/main-parties-in-uttar-pradesh-yet-to-release-manifestos-101642014353601.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===UPA===
INC released its manifesto in three tranches. The first tranche "''Shakti Vidhan''" was released on 19 December 2021 which centered on women welfare.<ref>{{cite web|title=कांग्रेस ने जारी किया शक्ति विधान महिला घोषणा 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220214131350/https://m.jagran.com/uttar-pradesh/gorakhpur-city-congress-has-released-shakti-vidhan-womens-manifesto-22308180.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The second tranche "''Bharti Vidhan''" was released on 21 January 2022 which centered on youth welfare.<ref>{{cite web|title=Indian National Congress Press Release Bharti Vidhan|url=https://www.inc.in/media/press-releases/bharti-vidhan-youth-manifesto-of-upcc|access-date=11 February 2022|website=Indian National Congress|language=en|archive-date=11 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220211075348/https://www.inc.in/media/press-releases/bharti-vidhan-youth-manifesto-of-upcc|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=UP Elections 2022 {{!}} Congress Releases Youth Manifesto|url=https://www.outlookindia.com/national/up-elections-2022-congress-releases-youth-manifesto-all-you-need-to-know-news-44820|access-date=11 February The final tranche "''Unnati Vidhan''" was released on 9 February 2022 which centered on overall development and general issues.<ref>{{cite web|title=UP Congress Manifesto 2022: प्रियंका ने जारी किया Some key promises<ref name="Congress Manifesto">{{cite web|title=मेनिफेस्टो (घोषणापत्र)|url=https://uttarpradeshcongress.in/manifesto|access-date=25 February 2022|website=भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस उत्तर प्रदेश|language=hi|archive-date=19 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220219064417/https://uttarpradeshcongress.in/manifesto/|url-status=live}}</ref> made in the manifesto are: 
* All farmers' debts shall be waived.
* Paddy and wheat shall be procured at ₹2,500 per quintal and sugarcane shall be procured at ₹400 per quintal
* Electricity bills shall be reduced by half and pending arrears from the COVID-19 period shall be waived
* ₹25,000 shall be provided to families worst-affected by COVID-19
* The backlog of 20 lakh jobs in the public sector shall be filled, including 8 lakh jobs for women
* Every girl in 10th and 12th standard will be given a smartphone and every woman enrolled in a graduate programme will be given an electric scooty
* 40% of Vidhan Sabha tickets will be given to women
*The fees for all examination forms will be waived and travel by bus and trains would be made free
*Fulfilment of all vacant seats in state healthcare hospitals
*Encouragement to industries, tourism, small and medium scale industries
*To boost startups in the state, a ‘Seed Startup Fund’ worth {{INRConvert|5000|c}} would be set up, prioritising the entrepreneurs below 30 years of age
*All the vacant posts for [[Sanskrit]], Urdu teachers [[Anganwadi]], [[Accredited Social Health Activist|Asha]] and so on will also be filled
* In the basic education sector, the shortage of one lakh head teachers will be met

===SP+===
Samajwadi Party released its manifesto on 8 February 2022.<ref>{{cite web|title=SP Manifesto 2022: यूपी में सपा सरकार बनी तो क्या-क्या मिलेगा मुफ्त, ये हैं अखिलेश यादव के 22 वादे|url=https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-samajwadi-party-manifesto-2022-akhilesh-yadav-promise-for-up-election-5767871.html|access-date=11 February 2022|website=Hindustan|language=hindi|archive-date=11 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220211085803/https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-samajwadi-party-manifesto-2022-akhilesh-yadav-promise-for-up-election-5767871.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

The National President of Samajwadi Party [[Akhilesh Yadav]] released his party's 88-page manifesto for the elections. Under the motto "''Satya Vachan, Atoot Vaada''" ({{Translation|Truth and Unbroken promises}}), the manifesto is laden with promises to farmers, women and the youth. Some key points from the party's manifesto are:

* Minimum support price (MSP) for all crops
* All farmers to be debt-free by 2025
* Free power for irrigation, interest-free loan and insurance as well as pension benefits to farmers
* {{INRConvert|25|l}} compensation to kin of farmers who died during farm protests
* Efforts to provide 22 lakh jobs in IT sector
* Urban employment guarantee act along lines of MGNREGS to boost jobs
* 33 per cent reservation for women of all communities (SCs/STs/General) in government jobs including police force
*Microfinance bank to help micro, small and medium enterprises (MSME)
* 300 units of free power to domestic consumers
* Free [[Wi-Fi]] zones in all villages and cities
* Further modernisation and upgradation of police and health infrastructure
* Dial 1890 Mazdoor Power Line- will be launched for migrant labourers
* Zero tolerance for organised crimes and hate crimes against women, minorities and Dalits<ref>{{cite web|title=UP polls: Debt-free farmers to 33% quota for women, key promises in Akhilesh's manifesto|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election/debtfree-farmers-to-33-quota-for-women-key-promises-in-akhilesh-s-manifesto-101644317368172.html|access-date=8 February 2022|website=Hindustan Times|date=8 February 2022|language=en|archive-date=8 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220208124024/https://www.hindustantimes.com/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election/debtfree-farmers-to-33-quota-for-women-key-promises-in-akhilesh-s-manifesto-101644317368172.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

== Campaigns ==
[[Samyukt Kisan Morcha]] (SKM), the umbrella body of farmers, campaigned against the ruling BJP by organising public meetings and rallies asking farmers to not vote for BJP.<ref name="Farmers BBC 4 Feb 2022">{{cite news |title=यूपी चुनाव में योगी आदित्यनाथ और अमित शाह की नई टेंशन क्या है? |url=https://www.bbc.com/hindi/india-60257089 |access-date=8 February 2022 |work=BBC News हिंदी |date=4 February 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=8 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220208153949/https://www.bbc.com/hindi/india-60257089 |url-status=live}}</ref> SKM had organised the [[2020–2021 Indian farmers' protest]] against the controversial [[2020 Indian agriculture acts|three farm acts]] which were passed by the BJP-led [[Union Government of India|Union Government]] in the BJP controlled [[Parliament of India]] in September 2020. These laws were eventually withdrawn by the Union government.<ref name="Farmers BBC 4 Feb 2022" />

On 31 January 2022, the farmer leaders observed "''Vishwasghat Diwas''" (treachery day) across India after the Union government failed to fulfil promises that were made to the farmers during the withdrawal of agitation against three farm laws. SKM leaders have warned that the farm laws may be re-introduced if BJP wins the elections.<ref>{{cite news |title=Anti-farmer laws may be back if BJP returns to power: SKM |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/antifarmer-laws-may-be-back-if-bjp-returns-to-power-skm-101644421294182.html |access-date=9 February 2022 |work=Hindustan Times |date=9 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=9 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209154854/https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/antifarmer-laws-may-be-back-if-bjp-returns-to-power-skm-101644421294182.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

According to the SKM leaders the promises that BJP made to the farmers in the 2022 manifesto were also made in the 2017 election manifesto, but they were not implemented. Neither the income of the farmer was doubled nor they got Minimum support Price (MSP) for their produce.<ref name="HT 9 Feb Anti Farmer">{{cite news |title=Anti-farmer laws may be back if BJP returns to power: SKM |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/antifarmer-laws-may-be-back-if-bjp-returns-to-power-skm-101644421294182.html |access-date=11 February 2022 |work=Hindustan Times |date=9 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=10 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220210231011/https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/antifarmer-laws-may-be-back-if-bjp-returns-to-power-skm-101644421294182.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

SKM leaders launched "Mission UP and Uttarakhand" and appealed to the voters to not vote for BJP calling them anti-farmer. The appeal did not make any recommendation to vote for any specific political party. SKM's appeal was supported by 57 farmer organisations. Since the campaign rallies were banned due to [[COVID-19 pandemic in India]], leaflets with the appeal were handed to the villagers.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Chatterjee |first1=Mohua |title='Don't vote for anti-farmer BJP', SKM leaders appeal to voters in UP, Uttarakhand |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/dont-vote-for-anti-farmer-bjp-skm-leaders-appeal-to-voters-in-up-uttarakhand/articleshow/89344867.cms |access-date=9 February 2022 |work=The Times of India |date=4 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=9 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209204110/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/dont-vote-for-anti-farmer-bjp-skm-leaders-appeal-to-voters-in-up-uttarakhand/articleshow/89344867.cms |url-status=live}}</ref>

=== Policy positions ===

==== Farm laws ====
BJP's Union Agriculture Minister [[Narendra Singh Tomar]] in December 2021, had said that BJP brought the [[2020 Indian agriculture acts|3 agriculture amendment laws]] (repealed in 2021). "But the government is not disappointed. We moved a step back and we will move forward again because farmers are India's backbone."<ref>{{cite news |title=Centre will bring back farm laws after Assembly polls, says Congress |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/centre-hatching-conspiracy-to-bring-back-farm-laws-after-assembly-polls-congress/article38037085.ece |access-date=9 February 2022 |work=The Hindu |date=25 December 2021 |language=en-IN |archive-date=9 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209172730/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/centre-hatching-conspiracy-to-bring-back-farm-laws-after-assembly-polls-congress/article38037085.ece |url-status=live}}</ref>

The [[Indian National Congress|INC]] and [[Aam Aadmi Party|AAP]] are against the farm laws and had supported the farmers' unions during their year-long protest against the farm laws.<ref>{{cite news |title=Farm laws like death warrants that must be taken back, says AAP's Sanjay Singh |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/farm-laws-like-death-warrants-that-must-be-taken-back-says-aap-s-sanjay-singh-101612033647503.html |access-date=9 February 2022 |work=Hindustan Times |date=31 January 2021 |language=en |archive-date=9 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209203003/https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/farm-laws-like-death-warrants-that-must-be-taken-back-says-aap-s-sanjay-singh-101612033647503.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=19 November 2021 |title=Farm laws: Spotlight now on next step as Congress, CPI(M) hail protesters |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/farm-laws-spotlight-now-on-next-step-as-congress-cpi-m-hail-protesters-101637299026857.html |access-date=11 February 2022 |website=Hindustan Times |language=en |archive-date=11 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220211091222/https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/farm-laws-spotlight-now-on-next-step-as-congress-cpi-m-hail-protesters-101637299026857.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

[[Samajwadi Party]] chief [[Akhilesh Yadav]] has stated that after forming the government in [[Uttar Pradesh]], SP will not allow the implementation of any anti-farmer laws in [[Uttar Pradesh]].<ref name="SP 28 Jan ET">{{cite news |title=Be wary of BJP, they withdrew farm laws for sake of votes: Akhilesh Yadav to farmers |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/be-wary-of-bjp-they-withdrew-farm-laws-for-sake-of-votes-akhilesh-yadav-to-farmers/articleshow/89184278.cms |access-date=9 February 2022 |work=The Economic Times |date=28 January 2022 |archive-date=9 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220209162933/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/be-wary-of-bjp-they-withdrew-farm-laws-for-sake-of-votes-akhilesh-yadav-to-farmers/articleshow/89184278.cms |url-status=live}}</ref> He warned the farmers against [[BJP]], and said that [[BJP]] had withdrawn the controversial farm laws to get votes from the farmers. SP's alliance partner Rashtriya Lok Dal is also against the implementation of farm laws.<ref name="SP 28 Jan ET" />

=== Election issues ===
Citing a survey from all constituencies of UP, DNA news reported that unemployment was the biggest issue for 73% of the voters. Inflation and price rise was second with 65% voter support. Development was 54% and stray animal was an issue for 39% voters.<ref name="DNA India">{{cite news |title=UP Election 2022 Opinion Poll: Here are the issues shaping votes in Uttar Pradesh |url=https://www.dnaindia.com/india/report-up-election-2022-opinion-poll-here-are-the-issues-shaping-votes-in-uttar-pradesh-assembly-bjp-sp-2929244 |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=DNA India |language=en |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220115542/https://www.dnaindia.com/india/report-up-election-2022-opinion-poll-here-are-the-issues-shaping-votes-in-uttar-pradesh-assembly-bjp-sp-2929244 |url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Stray cow ====
[[File:Cow in Varanasi street (8527193622).jpg|thumb|Stray cow on a busy street in [[Varanasi]]]]
[[Cattle slaughter in India|Slaughter of cow]] is illegal in most places in the country.<ref name="Gaushalas">{{Cite journal |title=The Management of Cow Shelters (Gaushalas) in India, Including the Attitudes of Shelter Managers to Cow Welfare |first1=Arvind |last1=Sharma |first2=Catherine |last2=Schuetze |first3=Clive J.C. |last3=Phillips |date=28 January 2020 |journal=Animals |volume=10 |issue=2 |page=211 |doi=10.3390/ani10020211 |pmid=32012807 |pmc=7070297 |doi-access=free}}</ref>  The anti slaughter laws were not strictly enforced until 2014.<ref name="barabanki">{{cite news |title=योगी की रैली के पहले छोड़े सैकड़ों सांड |url=https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/barabanki/news/barabanki-cm-rally-barabanki-political-news-barabanki-stray-animal-in-cm-rally-barabanki-latest-news-up-news-129428137.html |work=Dainik Bhaskar |date=22 February 2022 |access-date=23 February 2022 |archive-date=23 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220223010935/https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/barabanki/news/barabanki-cm-rally-barabanki-political-news-barabanki-stray-animal-in-cm-rally-barabanki-latest-news-up-news-129428137.html |url-status=live}}</ref> [[Cows]] are considered [[Cow in Hinduism|holy in Hinduism]], yet the farmers regularly took their old cows to slaughter houses until the years before 2014 when BJP's Narendra Modi government won the general election.<ref name="BBC UP deadly cow" />

The deadly [[stray cow]] attacks on humans and crops in both the urban and rural areas is an issue for the residents. In 2017, after coming to power, the [[First Yogi Adityanath ministry|Yogi Adityanath government]] had promised to build cattle shelters to better manage the stray cattle.<ref name="IT 11 Jan" /> Since BJP came to power in 2017, cow slaughter has been made illegal in 18 states in India including UP.<ref name="BBC UP deadly cow">{{cite news |title=Uttar Pradesh: Why deadly cow attacks are an issue in Indian state election |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-60108274 |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=BBC News |date=25 January 2022 |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220120315/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-60108274 |url-status=live}}</ref> Since then the trading of male cattle has reduced due to the fear of arrest, persecution, lynching by [[Cow vigilante violence in India|cow vigilantes]]. The farmers, unable to sell them, abandon them to wander on the roads and feed on standing crops.<ref>{{cite news |title=Stray cows become election issue in Uttar Pradesh |url=https://www.deccanherald.com/video/election/uttar-pradesh/stray-cows-become-election-issue-in-uttar-pradesh-1083050.html |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=Deccan Herald |date=19 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220113956/https://www.deccanherald.com/video/election/uttar-pradesh/stray-cows-become-election-issue-in-uttar-pradesh-1083050.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="NDTV UP Issues" /><ref>{{cite news |title=Stray cattle poll issue again but no one knows if it will affect the UP outcome |url=https://www.news9live.com/india/stray-cattle-poll-issue-again-but-no-one-knows-if-it-will-affect-the-up-outcome-152471 |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=NEWS9LIVE |date=9 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220121351/https://www.news9live.com/india/stray-cattle-poll-issue-again-but-no-one-knows-if-it-will-affect-the-up-outcome-152471 |url-status=live}}</ref>

The Adityanath ministry in the state of [[Uttar Pradesh]] introduced a special [[alcohol tax]] to earn money and maintain thousands of cow sheds operated by the government. The ''[[BBC]]'' reported that the tax did not solve the problem of stray [[cow]] and the cow sheds were found to be over crowded.<ref name="BBC UP deadly cow" />

BJP state government claims to have provided money to the village pradhans to set up cow shelters to keep the stray cattle. ''[[Deccan Herald]]'' reported that it could not find cattle shelters in the villages of [[Sitapur]], [[Lakhimpur Kheri]], [[Hardoi]] and [[Unnao]] districts. Places that had cow shelters, were already full with no capacity to keep more cattle.<ref name="Deccan Herald">{{cite news |title=Stray cattle may devour saffron votes in UP |url=https://www.deccanherald.com/election/uttar-pradesh/stray-cattle-may-devour-saffron-votes-in-up-1083041.html |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=Deccan Herald |date=19 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220113954/https://www.deccanherald.com/election/uttar-pradesh/stray-cattle-may-devour-saffron-votes-in-up-1083041.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

BJP leaders refused to accept that the stray cattle was an issue, even though the farmers consider it an issue.<ref name="Deccan Herald" /> Speaking at a rally in Kanpur in February, PM [[Narendra Modi]] acknowledged the problem in his rally and said the Yogi government is trying to solve the problem by setting up cattle shelters.<ref name="NDTV UP Issues">{{cite news |title=Rural UP's Biggest Election Issue – The Mighty Bull |url=https://www.ndtv.com/blog/rural-ups-biggest-election-issue-the-mighty-bull-2777077 |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=NDTV.com |date=19 February 2022 |archive-date=19 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220219215657/https://www.ndtv.com/blog/rural-ups-biggest-election-issue-the-mighty-bull-2777077 |url-status=live}}</ref>

The Samajwadi Party promised compensation of 5 Lakh ₹ for farmers who were killed by bulls.<ref name="IT 11 Jan">{{cite news |last1=Sultanpur/Gorakhpur/Siddharthnagar |first1=Ashutosh Mishra |title=Stray animals destroy crops in UP districts as goshalas remain non-starters |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/stray-animals-crops-up-districts-goshalas-non-starters-1898440-2022-01-11 |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=India Today |date=11 January 2022 |language=en |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220121349/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/stray-animals-crops-up-districts-goshalas-non-starters-1898440-2022-01-11 |url-status=live}}</ref> SP promised to fix the root cause of the problem, removing the fear in trading of livestock without any trouble or harassment.<ref name="NDTV UP Issues" /> [[Akhilesh Yadav]] said that the farmers were forced to guard their crops to prevent it from being grazen by stray animals. He said that his government will make arrangements to take care of the cattle.<ref name="NDTV 26 February 2022" />

The [[Uttar Pradesh Congress Committee|Congress Party]] promised<ref name="Congress Manifesto" /> compensation of ₹3,000 per acre for damaged crops. It also promised a [[Godhan Nyay Yojna|Godhan Nyay Yojana]] along the lines of a similar scheme in [[Chhattisgarh]] including the purchase of cow dung (gobar) for ₹2 per kilogram to promote organic farming and vermi-composting by [[Self-help group (finance)|Self-Help Groups]]. It promised an amount of ₹500 per stray animal that is turned into a village [[Goshala|Gaushala]] for a fixed period.

On 22 February, the local farmers released hundreds of cattle at the venue of the election rally in [[Barabanki, Uttar Pradesh|Barabanki]] located 40 km from the state capital [[Lucknow]]. The act was to highlight the menace of the stray cattle in the area. In a [[viral video]] of the incident, hundreds of unattended cattle were seen roaming in the open rally ground.<ref name="Adityanath rally Cow">{{cite news |title=Watch: UP Farmers Release Stray Cattle Near Yogi Adityanath's Rally Venue |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/watch-up-farmers-release-stray-cattle-near-yogi-adityanaths-rally-venue-2783182 |access-date=23 February 2022 |date=22 February 2022 |work=NDTV.com |archive-date=23 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220223034443/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/watch-up-farmers-release-stray-cattle-near-yogi-adityanaths-rally-venue-2783182 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="barabanki" /><ref>{{cite news |title=बाराबंकी में योगी की जनसभा से पहले मैदान में जुटे आवारा पशु, वीडियो वायरल |url=https://navbharattimes.indiatimes.com/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/video/stray-animals-gathered-in-field-before-yogis-public-meeting-in-barabanki-video-viral/videoshow/89754487.cms |access-date=23 February 2022 |work=Navbharat Times |language=hi |archive-date=23 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220223102701/https://navbharattimes.indiatimes.com/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/video/stray-animals-gathered-in-field-before-yogis-public-meeting-in-barabanki-video-viral/videoshow/89754487.cms |url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Roads ====
Lack of roads was a major poll issue for people in [[Amethi]].<ref name="Amethi Wire">{{cite news |title=UP Polls: In Amethi, Voters Seek Solutions for Stray Cattle Menace, Flooding, Bad Roads |url=https://thewire.in/politics/up-polls-amethi-voters-seek-solutions-stray-cattle-menace-flooding-bad-roads |access-date=20 February 2022 |work=The Wire |date=19 February 2022 |archive-date=20 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220220114552/https://thewire.in/politics/up-polls-amethi-voters-seek-solutions-stray-cattle-menace-flooding-bad-roads |url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Petrol diesel price ====
[[Akhilesh Yadav]], [[Rahul Gandhi]], [[Priyanka Gandhi Vadra]] campaigned on the massive increase in the price of petrol and diesel under the BJP government. [[Akhilesh]] claimed that soon after the UP elections are over the government will increase the price even more.<ref name="NDTV 26 February 2022">{{cite news |title=BJP Will Hike Petrol Price After Elections Are Over: Akhilesh Yadav |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/akhilesh-slams-bjp-over-inflation-claims-govt-set-to-increase-fuel-price-after-polls-2789507 |access-date=6 March 2022 |work=NDTV.com |date=26 February 2022 |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306202157/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/akhilesh-slams-bjp-over-inflation-claims-govt-set-to-increase-fuel-price-after-polls-2789507 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Tribune News 5 March 2022">{{cite news |title=It's curtains on campaign; PM Modi, Akhilesh, Priyanka make final push |url=https://www.tribuneindia.com/news/nation/its-curtains-on-campaign-pm-modi-akhilesh-priyanka-make-final-push-375410 |access-date=6 March 2022 |work=Tribuneindia News Service |date=5 March 2022 |language=en |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306010128/https://www.tribuneindia.com/news/nation/its-curtains-on-campaign-pm-modi-akhilesh-priyanka-make-final-push-375410 |url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Inflation ====
[[Akhilesh Yadav]] stated that public will not be hit with inflation if SP alliance wins the election.<ref name="outlookindia Inflation">{{cite news |title='Govt Set To Hike Fuel Prices After Elections': Akhilesh Yadav Slams BJP Over Inflation |url=https://www.outlookindia.com/national/akhilesh-yadav-claims-govt-is-set-to-hike-fuel-prices-post-elections-castigates-bjp-over-inflation-news-184045 |access-date=6 March 2022 |work=outlookindia.com/ |date=25 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306105400/https://www.outlookindia.com/national/akhilesh-yadav-claims-govt-is-set-to-hike-fuel-prices-post-elections-castigates-bjp-over-inflation-news-184045 |url-status=live}}</ref>

==== Unemployment ====
[[Akhilesh Yadav]] stated that he will give jobs to people and end the problem of unemployment.<ref name="NDTV 26 February 2022" /> He also promised early recruitment for the Police and Army.<ref name="Tribune News 5 March 2022" />

==== COVID-19 ====
The government's handling of the [[COVID-19 pandemic in India]] was criticised by [[Priyanka Gandhi]], [[Arvind Kejriwal]] and [[Akhilesh Yadav]] who stated that the people were not given the needed help. Akhilesh accused the BJP government of hiding the actual data and fudging the data of Covid related deaths.<ref name="Hindustan Times fudged coronavirus">{{cite news |title=UP govt fudged coronavirus fatality number, alleges Akhilesh Yadav |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/up-govt-fudged-coronavirus-fatality-number-alleges-akhilesh-yadav-101619440682098.html |access-date=6 March 2022 |work=Hindustan Times |date=26 April 2021 |language=en |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306202157/https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/lucknow-news/up-govt-fudged-coronavirus-fatality-number-alleges-akhilesh-yadav-101619440682098.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="coronavirus death">{{cite news |title=Akhilesh Yadav alleges UP govt hiding actual coronavirus death toll |url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/current-affairs/akhilesh-yadav-alleges-up-govt-hiding-actual-coronavirus-death-toll-121062200450_1.html |access-date=6 March 2022 |work=Business Standard India |agency=Press Trust of India |date=22 June 2021 |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306202157/https://www.business-standard.com/article/current-affairs/akhilesh-yadav-alleges-up-govt-hiding-actual-coronavirus-death-toll-121062200450_1.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

==Incidents==
===Voting Machine malfunction===
In the first, third and fourth phase of election, [[Electronic voting in India|Electronic Voter Machine (EVM)]] malfunction was reported at several places.<ref>{{cite news |title=Assembly Elections 2022 Live Updates {{!}} Money extracted from mafia being used for welfare schemes: UP CM at rally |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/story/assembly-elections-2022-live-updates-uttar-pradesh-manipur-uttarakhand-punjab-goa-1917058-2022-02-24 |access-date=24 February 2022 |work=India Today |date=24 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=24 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220224022302/https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/story/assembly-elections-2022-live-updates-uttar-pradesh-manipur-uttarakhand-punjab-goa-1917058-2022-02-24 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=UP Election 2022: सपा ने लगाया कई जगह EVM खराब होने का आरोप |url=https://livebharatnews.in/up-election-2022/UP-Election-2022-SP-alleges-EVM-malfunction-at-many-places/cid6496944.htm |access-date=24 February 2022 |work=livebharatnews.in |date=23 February 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=24 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220224074918/https://livebharatnews.in/up-election-2022/UP-Election-2022-SP-alleges-EVM-malfunction-at-many-places/cid6496944.htm |url-status=live }}</ref> Talking about the first phase of elections, the Additional Chief Election Officer said that the malfunctioning machines were replaced after reports of malfunctions.<ref name="First Phase 10 February">{{cite news |title=UP polls: Over 35% voting till 1 pm; EVM glitches reported from some booths |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/up-polls-35-voting-till-1-pm-evm-glitches-reported-some-booths-1911297-2022-02-10 |access-date=27 February 2022 |work=India Today |agency=Press Trust of India |issue=Lucknow |date=10 February 2022 |language=en |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309050442/https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/up-polls-35-voting-till-1-pm-evm-glitches-reported-some-booths-1911297-2022-02-10 |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Unattended VVPAT slips ===
On 4 March, in [[Basti, Uttar Pradesh]], a large number of [[Voter-verified paper audit trail|VVPAT]] slips were found unattended on ground near the strong room where the EVMs were kept prior to counting.<ref name="NBT 4 March">{{cite news |title=UP Chunav: वोटिंग के बाद स्ट्रांग रूम के पास मिलीं VVPAT पर्चियां, निष्पक्ष चुनाव को लेकर खड़े हुए सवाल |url=https://navbharattimes.indiatimes.com/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/news/vvpat-slip-found-in-basti-near-strong-room-up-election-2022-hindi-update/articleshow/89997291.cms |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=Navbharat Times |date=4 March 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308173708/https://navbharattimes.indiatimes.com/elections/assembly-elections/uttar-pradesh/news/vvpat-slip-found-in-basti-near-strong-room-up-election-2022-hindi-update/articleshow/89997291.cms |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Hindustan Basti 4 March">{{cite news |title=बस्‍ती में बच्‍चों को मिलीं वीवीपैट से निकली पर्चियां और EVM एड्रेस टैग, DM ने दी ये सफाई |url=https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-children-got-vvpat-slips-evm-address-tags-from-outside-mandi-samiti-strong-room-basti-dm-clarification-5960028.html |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=Hindustan |date=4 March 2022 |language=hindi |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308173709/https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-children-got-vvpat-slips-evm-address-tags-from-outside-mandi-samiti-strong-room-basti-dm-clarification-5960028.html |url-status=live }}</ref> The slips were found by children out for playing. After the news of discovery of slips spread, a large crowd gathered that included party members and candidates along with members of the administration. According to the district officials, those slips may have been from the mock drill before election but admitted they should not have been thrown out. The administration assured investigation into the incident and action on the people involved.<ref name="abplive 4 March 2022">{{cite news |last1=श्रीवास्तव |first1=सतीश |title=बस्ती में स्ट्रॉन्ग रूम के पीछे वीवीपैट की पर्चियां मिलने से मचा हड़कंप, SDM ने दी ये जानकारी |url=https://www.abplive.com/elections/up-assembly-election-2022-vvpat-slips-found-behind-strong-room-in-basti-ann-2074633 |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=www.abplive.com |agency=एबीपी न्यूज़ |date=4 March 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308173709/https://www.abplive.com/elections/up-assembly-election-2022-vvpat-slips-found-behind-strong-room-in-basti-ann-2074633 |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Voting Machine theft===
On 8 March 2022, in Varanasi, a truck carrying multiple EVMs was caught by the members of the Samajwadi Party in Varanasi. Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav said that SP members had caught one truck while two other trucks fled. According to the guidelines of Election Commission (EC) the EVMs cannot be moved without the consent of the contesting candidates, yet the Varanasi [[District Magistrate]] (DM) was transporting them without informing the local candidates.<ref name="Wire EVM 8 March 2022" /> Yadav alleged that the Electronic Voting machines caught were being stolen from a EVM counting centre in Varanasi. The incident had occurred two days before the scheduled counting of votes on 10 March.<ref name="EVM Truck 8 March 2022">{{cite news |title=Varanasi Video Has Trucks 'Stealing' Voting Machines: Akhilesh Yadav |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/48-hours-before-counting-akhilesh-yadavs-voting-machine-charge-in-up-2811008 |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=NDTV.com |date=8 March 2022 |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308133956/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/48-hours-before-counting-akhilesh-yadavs-voting-machine-charge-in-up-2811008 |url-status=live }}</ref> Videos of the incident were also circulated on social media.<ref name="EVM Truck">{{cite web |title=Video of EVM Truck |url=https://twitter.com/SwamiPMaurya/status/1501183728755769352 |website=Twitter |access-date=8 March 2022 |language=en |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308131145/https://twitter.com/SwamiPMaurya/status/1501183728755769352 |url-status=live }}</ref>

The DM of Varanasi said that the EVMs in the incident were the ones used for training. Yadav said, "Now that the EVMs have been caught, the officials will make multiple excuses". He called the candidates and party supporters to be alert to prevent any attempt of rigging during the vote counting. The SP+ alliance complained about the incident to the Election commission (EC). The EC had assured action in the incident.<ref name="EVM Truck 8 March 2022" /> Yadav addressed a press conference where he accused the Election Commission officials of EVM tampering and added that he had lost trust on the EC. He said "We need to be alert if EVMs are being transported this way. This is theft. We need to save our votes. We may go to court against it but before that, I want to appeal to people to save democracy."<ref name="Wire EVM 8 March 2022">{{cite news |title=UP: Akhilesh Yadav Alleges 'EVM Fraud', 'Vote Theft' in Varanasi |url=https://thewire.in/politics/up-akhilesh-yadav-alleges-evm-fraud-vote-theft-in-varanasi |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=The Wire |date=8 March 2022 |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308150659/https://thewire.in/politics/up-akhilesh-yadav-alleges-evm-fraud-vote-theft-in-varanasi |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="EVM Tamper 8 March 2022">{{cite news |title=EVMs being tampered, says Akhilesh Yadav ahead of UP poll results |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/evm-ec-varanasi-akhilesh-yadav-up-assembly-poll-results-1922345-2022-03-08 |access-date=8 March 2022 |work=India Today |date=8 March 2022 |language=en |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308141257/https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/uttar-pradesh-assembly-polls-2022/story/evm-ec-varanasi-akhilesh-yadav-up-assembly-poll-results-1922345-2022-03-08 |url-status=live }}</ref>

Varanasi police commissioner had admitted to lapses in the incident. Samajwadi Party had shared the video of the admission calling it an admission that EC protocol had not been followed by the officials. The Election Commission ordered action against Varanasi Additional District Magistrate and suspended him from his post for violating the rules in transportation of EVMs. According to the District Magistrate the EVMs were planned to be moved in the morning of 9 January, but the suspended Additional District Magistrate had taken them out in the previous night without providing the necessary information to others.<ref name="Suspended 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=Varanasi Officer To Be Suspended After Akhilesh Yadav's "EVM Chori" Video |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/varanasi-officer-suspended-after-akhilesh-yadavs-evm-chori-video-2812726 |work=NDTV.com |date=9 March 2022 |access-date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309164041/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/varanasi-officer-suspended-after-akhilesh-yadavs-evm-chori-video-2812726 |url-status=live }}</ref>

=== Postal ballot in garbage truck===
On 9 March, in Bareilly three boxes full of election voting material (blank postal ballot) were discovered in the garbage trucks of city municipality. The truck was in the warehouse where voting machines and ballot papers were kept.<ref>{{cite news |last1=मिश्रा |first1=अनूप कुमार |title=बरेली में कूड़े की गाड़ी में मिले बैलेट पेपर, सपा नेता और प्रशासन के पहुंचने पर मचा हड़कंप |url=https://www.abplive.com/states/up-uk/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election-2022-bareilly-ballot-paper-out-side-counting-center-samajwadi-party-ann-2077136 |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=abplive.com |date=9 March 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309013937/https://www.abplive.com/states/up-uk/uttar-pradesh-assembly-election-2022-bareilly-ballot-paper-out-side-counting-center-samajwadi-party-ann-2077136 |url-status=live }}</ref> The incident video was viral in the social media.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bareilly Ballot box in garbage truck video |url=https://twitter.com/srinivasiyc/status/1501260580983767041 |website=Twitter |access-date=9 March 2022 |language=en |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308181659/https://twitter.com/srinivasiyc/status/1501260580983767041 |url-status=live }}</ref> Police and district administration arrived to meet Samajwadi party members regarding the incident and assured to take actions on their complaint.<ref name="Bareily 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=ईवीएम पर बवालः बरेली और बनारस के डीएम से मांगी रिपोर्ट, चुनाव आयोग को भेजेंगे |url=https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-ruckus-on-evm-report-sought-from-dm-of-bareilly-and-banaras-will-send-to-election-commission-5985958.html |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=Hindustan |date=9 March 2022 |language=hindi |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308193617/https://www.livehindustan.com/uttar-pradesh/story-ruckus-on-evm-report-sought-from-dm-of-bareilly-and-banaras-will-send-to-election-commission-5985958.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Prabhat Khabar 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=UP Election Result 2022: बरेली में मतगणना से पहले EVM में धांधली का आरोप, DM ने जांच का दिया आश्वासन |url=https://www.prabhatkhabar.com/election/up-assembly-elections/up-election-result-2022-evm-rigging-will-be-investigated-in-bareilly-sht |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=Prabhat Khabar - Hindi News |date=9 March 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309034207/https://www.prabhatkhabar.com/election/up-assembly-elections/up-election-result-2022-evm-rigging-will-be-investigated-in-bareilly-sht |url-status=live }}</ref> The Election commission acted on the complaint and removed the involved election official along with the SDM from their post.<ref name="Dainik Jagran SDM 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=बरेली में कूड़े की गाड़ी में पोस्टल बैलेट ले जाने पर हटाई गईं एसडीएम, सपाइयों के हंगामे के बाद हुई कार्रवाई |url=https://www.jagran.com/uttar-pradesh/bareilly-city-up-election-2022-postal-ballot-found-in-garbage-cart-in-bareilly-sdm-removed-from-post-22530620.html |work=Dainik Jagran |date=9 March 2022 |language=hi |access-date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309133724/https://www.jagran.com/uttar-pradesh/bareilly-city-up-election-2022-postal-ballot-found-in-garbage-cart-in-bareilly-sdm-removed-from-post-22530620.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

On the same day in Moradabad 2 boxes of ballot paper was found by SP members in the truck operated by municipality.<ref name="News18 Muradabad 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=बरेली के बाद अब मुरादाबाद में मिले बैलट पेपर से भरे 2 बड़े बक्से, सपाइयों का हंगामा और धक्का-मुक्की |url=https://hindi.news18.com/news/uttar-pradesh/moradabad-two-ballot-paper-box-found-in-municipal-council-bilari-vehicle-in-mooradaba-after-bareilly-varanasi-evm-row-4058305.html |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=News18 हिंदी |date=9 March 2022 |language=hi |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309115411/https://hindi.news18.com/news/uttar-pradesh/moradabad-two-ballot-paper-box-found-in-municipal-council-bilari-vehicle-in-mooradaba-after-bareilly-varanasi-evm-row-4058305.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

In [[Sonbhadra district]] a box full of ballot papers was found in a truck accompanied by SDM's vehicle. Both vehicles were stopped outside the ballot counting center when they were trying to enter. The SDM was accused by SP members of replacing the ballot papers.<ref name="Dainik Bhaskar rigging" /> Election commission ordered the SDM to be removed from his post.<ref name="satyahindi 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=ईवीएमः सोनभद्र के एसडीएम को हटाया, मुरादाबाद में हंगामा, अफसरों की गाड़ियां हो रही हैं चेक |url=https://www.satyahindi.com/politics/evm-complaints-increased-sonbhadra-s-sdm-removed-officers-vehicles-are-being-checked-125601.html |work=satyahindi.com |date=9 March 2022 |language=en |access-date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309120139/https://www.satyahindi.com/politics/evm-complaints-increased-sonbhadra-s-sdm-removed-officers-vehicles-are-being-checked-125601.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Vote rigging allegations ===
Controversy related to the security of the electronic voting machines happened in 10 districts of UP. In Lucknow members of all parties except ruling BJP were present to guard the voting machines.<ref name="bareillyratjaga">{{cite news|url=https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/bareilly/news/bareillyratjaga-for-evm-chowkidari-on-the-call-of-akhilesh-sp-leaders-pitched-their-tents-at-the-counting-site-and-played-drums-and-majira-129487607.html|date=9 March 2022|access-date=9 March 2022|title=बरेली में EVM चौकीदारी के लिए रतजगा:सपा नेताओं ने मतगणना स्थल पर गाड़ा तंबू, बजा रहे ढोल-मजीरा|archive-date=9 March 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309130922/https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/bareilly/news/bareillyratjaga-for-evm-chowkidari-on-the-call-of-akhilesh-sp-leaders-pitched-their-tents-at-the-counting-site-and-played-drums-and-majira-129487607.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Dainik Bhaskar Manjira">{{cite news |title=यूपी के 10 जिलों में EVM पर हंगामा:वाराणसी के कमिश्नर ने माना मूवमेंट में हुई गलती, उन्नाव में सील करने का सामान ले जा रहे लेखपाल को पकड़ा |url=https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/varanasi/news/in-many-cities-outside-the-counting-centers-throughout-the-night-samajwadi-party-workers-kept-awake-by-playing-the-dholak-manjira-129486529.html |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=Dainik Bhaskar |date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309061332/https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/varanasi/news/in-many-cities-outside-the-counting-centers-throughout-the-night-samajwadi-party-workers-kept-awake-by-playing-the-dholak-manjira-129486529.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Dainik Bhaskar rigging">{{cite news |title=बनारस में स्ट्रांग रूम के बाहर EVM मिलने पर बवाल:सपा कार्यकर्ताओं ने डीएम को घेरा, 15 थानों की फोर्स बुलाई; बरेली में कूड़े की गाड़ी में मिले बैलेट पेपर्स |url=https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/varanasi/news/sp-accuses-administration-of-rigging-in-varanasi-dm-said-machine-was-going-for-training-of-employees-129483714.html |access-date=9 March 2022 |work=Dainik Bhaskar |date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309003617/https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/varanasi/news/sp-accuses-administration-of-rigging-in-varanasi-dm-said-machine-was-going-for-training-of-employees-129483714.html |url-status=live }}</ref> In [[Jalaun]] a car that was frequently moving in and out of the strong room was checked by SP workers who found hardware tools in the car.<ref name="Dainik Bhaskar rigging" />

In Sultanpur a truck full of boxes of EVM was found near the strong room, storing voting equipments. The SP workers guarding the strong room refused to let the truck enter the premises. The district officials claimed the boxes were empty. After ruckus, the district authority ordered that no voting related equipments should be moved before 11 March (the counting of vote was scheduled on 10 March).<ref name="9 March 2022 Sultanpur">{{cite news |title=सुल्तानपुर में डीसीएम में ईवीएम मिलने पर हंगामा:मंडी परिसर के स्ट्रांग रूम के बाहर खड़ी थी गाड़ी, सपा कार्यताओं ने हंगामा किया, डीएम बोले- ईवीएम के खाली डिब्बे थे |url=https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/sultanpur/news/uproar-over-getting-evm-in-dcm-in-sultanpur-the-car-was-standing-outside-the-strong-room-of-the-mandi-complex-sp-workers-created-a-ruckus-dm-said-evms-were-empty-129484531.html |access-date=9 March 2022 |date=8 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309002220/https://www.bhaskar.com/local/uttar-pradesh/sultanpur/news/uproar-over-getting-evm-in-dcm-in-sultanpur-the-car-was-standing-outside-the-strong-room-of-the-mandi-complex-sp-workers-created-a-ruckus-dm-said-evms-were-empty-129484531.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

Akhilesh Yadav alleged that the Office of the UP Chief Minister was making attempts to influence the counting of votes.<ref name="Pioneer EVM 9 March 2022">{{cite news |title=CM Office trying to influence counting of votes: Akhilesh |url=https://www.dailypioneer.com/2022/state-editions/cm-office-trying-to--influence-counting--of-votes--akhilesh.html |work=The Pioneer |date=9 March 2022 |language=en |access-date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308192217/https://www.dailypioneer.com/2022/state-editions/cm-office-trying-to--influence-counting--of-votes--akhilesh.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

Election officer had asked details of the incident in Bareilly and Varanasi from the district administration for Election Commission to take a final decision on the incidents.<ref name="Bareily 9 March 2022" /> Election Commission suspended the Varanasi ADM, Bareilly SDM and Sonbhadra SDM from election related duties.<ref name="9 March 2022 Amar Ujala Bareilly">{{cite news |title=ईवीएम को लेकर यूपी में हंगामा : तीन जिलों के बड़े अफसर हटाए गए, चुनाव आयोग भी सख्त, पढ़िए अब तक क्या-क्या हुआ? |url=https://www.amarujala.com/uttar-pradesh/evm-controversy-up-election-result-2022-election-commission-action-on-adm-in-varanasi-sdm-sonbhadra-and-bareilly?pageId=1 |work=Amar Ujala |date=9 March 2022 |language=hi |access-date=9 March 2022 |archive-date=9 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220309180821/https://www.amarujala.com/uttar-pradesh/evm-controversy-up-election-result-2022-election-commission-action-on-adm-in-varanasi-sdm-sonbhadra-and-bareilly?pageId=1 |url-status=live }}</ref>

=== Exit polls ===
The Election Commission banned the media from publishing [[exit polls]] between 7 AM on 10 February 2022 and 6:30 PM on 7 March 2022. With an imprisonment of two years for the violation of the directive.<ref>{{cite news |title=EC bans exit polls in five election-bound states between February 10 and March 7 |url=https://scroll.in/latest/1016234/ec-bans-exit-polls-in-five-election-bound-states-between-february-10-and-march-7 |access-date=8 February 2022 |work=Scroll.in |archive-date=8 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220208191456/https://scroll.in/latest/1016234/ec-bans-exit-polls-in-five-election-bound-states-between-february-10-and-march-7 |url-status=live }}</ref> Accordingly, these exit polls were published in the evening of 7 March 2022.
